mnemonics.title: SDLC Phases
mnemonics.content: R-D-I-T-D-M: Requirements → Design → Implementation → Testing → Deployment → Maintenance (First letters form a memorable pattern)

mnemonics.title: Non-Functional Requirements
mnemonics.content: PURSER: Performance, Usability, Reliability, Security, Efficiency, Reusability (Quality attributes)

acronyms.usage: The complete process of developing software from requirements to maintenance
Document containing all requirements of the software system
Tools that automate software development process
Activities ensuring software meets quality standards

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a waterfall - water flows down from one level to the next, cannot flow back up. Each phase must complete before the next begins.

visualTips.visualize: Imagine a spiral starting from center, expanding outward in cycles. Each loop represents an iteration with risk analysis, planning, and development.
A staircase going down - each step is a phase. You must complete each step before moving to the next lower step.

analogies.analogy: Like an architect discussing with a client what building they want before drawing any plans.
Like a rough sketch or clay model of a product before final manufacturing.
Like regular servicing and repairs of a car after purchase to keep it running well.
Verification: checking if you followed the recipe correctly. Validation: tasting if the dish tastes good.
